name: Sierra de Na Burguesa y calas de es Carregador, Major y Portals nous — circular gravel from Palma de Mallorca
name_original: Sierra de Na Burguesa y calas de es Carregador, Major y Portals nous — circular gravel desde Palma de Mallorca
region_intro: Touring cycling routes around Palma offer diverse landscapes, from the challenging ascents of the Serra de Tramuntana mountains to the expansive, largely flat coastal paths. The region features well-maintained roads and varied terrain suitable for different cycling preferences. Central Mallorca provides rolling terrain with less elevation gain, while the coastline offers easy riding with views of the bay. Best touring cycling routes around Palma The most popular touring cycling route is C'an Pere Antoni Beach loop from Les Meravelles, a 20.5 miles (33.0 km) trail that takes 2 hours 3 minutes to complete. This route offers scenic coastal views along the bay. Another top favourite among local touring cyclists is View of Palma – C'an Pere Antoni Beach loop from Plaça d'Espanya, an easy 17.0 miles (27.4 km) path. This route provides views of Palma and access to the beach. Local touring cyclists also love the Castillo de Bellver and seaside promenade — circular route through Palma de Mallorca, a 25.8 miles (41.5 km) trail leading through urban landscapes and along the coast, often completed in about 2 hours 30 minutes. Touring cycling around Palma is defined by coastal paths, rolling plains, and mountain roads. The network offers options for different ability levels, from leisurely rides to challenging ascents. The routes in Palma are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3100 reviews. More than 36000 touring cyclists have used komoot to explore Palma's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many touring cycling routes are available around Palma?
A: Palma offers a wide selection of touring cycling routes, with over 390 options available. These routes cater to various skill levels, including easy, moderate, and challenging rides.

Q: What types of terrain can I expect on touring cycling routes in Palma?
A: The terrain around Palma is incredibly diverse. You'll find challenging ascents and breathtaking views in the &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Tramuntana&lt;/strong&gt; mountains, expansive and largely flat coastal paths along the &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Marítimo&lt;/strong&gt;, and rolling terrain with less elevation gain in the central plains. Iconic climbs like &lt;strong&gt;Coll de Randa&lt;/strong&gt; and &lt;strong&gt;Sa Calobra&lt;/strong&gt; also offer unique experiences.

Q: What is the best time of year for touring cycling in Palma?
A: Palma's mild climate makes it suitable for touring cycling for much of the year. The spring and autumn months generally offer the most pleasant weather, with comfortable temperatures and less extreme heat than summer, making it ideal for exploring the diverse landscapes.

Q: Are there easy touring cycling routes suitable for beginners or families in Palma?
A: Yes, Palma has numerous easy routes perfect for beginners and families. For instance, the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/32439162" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;View of Palma – C'an Pere Antoni Beach loop from Plaça d'Espanya&lt;/a&gt; is an easy 27.4 km path offering scenic views of Palma and access to the beach. The coastal paths along the Paseo Marítimo are also largely flat and well-maintained, ideal for leisurely rides.

Q: Are there challenging touring cycling routes for experienced cyclists in Palma?
A: Absolutely. Experienced cyclists can tackle the demanding ascents of the &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Tramuntana&lt;/strong&gt;, including routes that feature significant climbs like Puig Major. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1004052684" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Na Burguesa y calas de es Carregador, Major y Portals nous — circular gravel from Palma de Mallorca&lt;/a&gt; is a difficult 34 km route with substantial elevation gain, offering a rigorous challenge.

Q: What scenic landmarks or natural features can I see along the touring cycling routes?
A: Many routes offer stunning views and access to landmarks. You can cycle past the historic &lt;strong&gt;Bellver Castle&lt;/strong&gt;, explore the scenic &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Marítimo&lt;/strong&gt;, or visit the iconic &lt;strong&gt;La Seu (Palma Cathedral)&lt;/strong&gt;. The &lt;strong&gt;Serra de Tramuntana&lt;/strong&gt; provides breathtaking Mediterranean vistas, while routes to &lt;strong&gt;Cap de Formentor&lt;/strong&gt; lead to a famous lighthouse. You might also encounter natural highlights like &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/357199" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Scenic Road Through Rocky Landscape&lt;/a&gt; or &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/6457879" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Torrent de Valldemossa&lt;/a&gt;.

Q: Are there circular touring cycling routes around Palma?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes around Palma are circular, allowing you to start and end in the same location. An example is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1003919850/castillo-de-bellver-and-seaside-promenade-circular-route-through-palma-de-mallorca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Bellver and seaside promenade — circular route through Palma de Mallorca&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 41.5 km through urban landscapes and along the coast.

Q: Can I find cafes or restaurants along the touring cycling routes in Palma?
A: Yes, many touring cycling routes, especially those along the coast or passing through villages, offer opportunities to stop at cafes and restaurants. The &lt;strong&gt;Paseo Marítimo&lt;/strong&gt;, for example, is lined with various establishments, and traditional villages in the central plains like Sineu and Petra also provide charming spots for a break.

Q: What do other touring cyclists enjoy most about cycling in Palma?
A: The touring cycling routes in Palma are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.5 stars from over 3100 reviews. Cyclists often praise the diverse landscapes, from challenging mountain passes to easy coastal paths, and the well-maintained infrastructure that caters to all levels.

Q: Are there any specific permits required for touring cycling in Palma?
A: Generally, no specific permits are required for touring cycling on public roads and designated cycle paths around Palma. However, if you plan to cycle through private land or specific natural reserves, it's always advisable to check local regulations beforehand.

Q: Are there any touring cycling routes that lead to beaches?
A: Yes, several routes lead to beautiful beaches. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/31967033" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;C'an Pere Antoni Beach loop from Les Meravelles&lt;/a&gt; is a popular 33 km route offering scenic coastal views along the bay, including access to C'an Pere Antoni Beach. You can also find routes leading to &lt;strong&gt;Es Trenc Beach&lt;/strong&gt;, known for its crystal-clear waters.

Q: Can I use public transport with my bike in Palma?
A: Palma's public transport system, including buses and trains, often has restrictions or specific times for carrying bicycles, especially during peak hours. It's recommended to check the current policies of the local public transport providers (EMT Palma for buses, TIB for intercity buses and trains) for detailed information on bike carriage.

Q: Where can I find parking for cyclists near the touring routes in Palma?
A: Parking availability varies depending on the starting point of your chosen route. In Palma city, there are public parking garages, and some coastal areas might have dedicated parking lots. For routes starting in smaller towns or rural areas, street parking might be available, but it's always best to plan your starting point and check for parking options in advance.
tips: As of winter 2023, it's hardly passable on a rigid gravel bike with 40mm tires. There are heaps of loose stones and rocks where it's tough to maintain traction, especially uphill.
